title: New Zealand Health Survey | Ministry of Health NZ
content:
## New Zealand Health Survey
The New Zealand Health Survey (NZHS) provides information about the health and wellbeing of adults and children in New Zealand. The NZHS became a continuous survey in 2011, enabling the publication of annual updates on the health of New Zealanders.
- Testing an online version of the survey
- Information for participants
- 2024/25 New Zealand Health Survey
- Regional Data Release
- Access to survey microdata
The Annual Data Explorer presents the latest results on the health of New Zealanders from the 2024/25 New Zealand Health Survey . View results by gender, age group, ethnic group, neighbourhood deprivation, disability status, and the four New Zealand health regions and compare with results from previous years.
Annual Data Explorer north_east Regional Data Release
Information collected through the New Zealand Health Survey supports the development of health services, policy and strategy. Survey data is collected continuously but findings are reported annually.
The results provide data for people selected for the survey period from July to June each year.

## Access to survey microdata
Information for researchers about accessing survey microdata from the Stats NZ Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI) or as a confidentialised unit record file (CURF).

## Access to other health data
Health New Zealand – Te Whatu Ora publishes a list of data sources commonly used when analysing the health of New Zealand populations.
